acceptance_criteria: The USER regime no longer explodes mechanically through sovereign-interest transfers to household income.
Issuer/user comparisons report interpretable differences in output, effective spending, debt, and rate dynamics.
Tests cover the repaired mechanism and document what the model does not establish.
The experiment can be safely kept out of the main evidence chain until explicitly revalidated.
constraints: Do not assume the repaired model must support the blog's preferred direction; document whatever it actually shows.
Keep the current draft's evidence boundaries in place until a separate validation pass is done.
description: Refactor the USER regime treatment of sovereign interest expense, risk premia, and consolidation pressure so the output paths reflect financing constraints rather than explosive household transfer feedback.
goal: Repair or replace the issuer-versus-user SFC experiment so financing architecture differences are interpretable rather than dominated by interest-transfer artifacts.
implementation_notes: Superseded by task-018, which broadens the repaired simulation target from issuer-versus-user only to a full household-versus-user-versus-issuer constraint comparison.
title: Issuer-versus-user SFC redesign
